scout_profile.notes: Deep SEC champion. Physical frontcourt, lottery talent.
Izzo's tournament pedigree. Senior-led, grind-it-out style.
Elite defense, efficient offense. Big 12 contender.
SEC battle-tested. Underrated defensive team.
Dusty May's first full Big Ten season. Deep guard corps.
Turnover-prone but explosive offense. SEC upset pick.
High-energy press, 3-point reliant. High variance.
Young program under rebuild. Pat Kelsey 2nd year. Good energy.
Elite 3-point shooting team but can go cold. Upset risk both ways.
First-year coach from UC San Diego. First tournament. Dangerous 10-seed.
Inconsistent season but talented. UNC always dangerous in March.
New coach after Eric Olen left. Surprising Big West title. 12-seed upset risk.
James Jones tenure. Yale execution teams are always a threat.
Veteran coach, solid program. Too big a gap vs #3 seed.
Mid-major survivor. Long shot to pull R64 upset.
SWAC auto-bid. No realistic path vs Auburn.
Nation's best team. Freshman-heavy but elite talent.
Most 3PA rate in tournament. Feast or famine offense.
Elite shot selection, deliberate pace. Senior-led. Clutch FT shooting.
Tommy Lloyd system. Elite offensive efficiency in Big 12.
Dana Altman's trademark transition attack. Deep tournament experience.
Kevin Young bringing NBA-style spacing. Second-year momentum.
Bennett's system: deliberate, precise, senior-led. Always dangerous.
SEC toughness. Survives on defense in close games.
Scott Drew's system. 2021 champion pedigree. R64 always dangerous.
SEC product. Close-game experience in tough league.
HAVOC defense. Force turnovers, run in transition. 11-seed danger zone.
Liberty perimeter shooting. Genuine 12-seed upset threat vs Oregon.
Groce's experience. MAC champion. Solid R64 upset chance vs Arizona.
Big Sky's best team. Long shot vs Wisconsin.
Scrappy Horizon team. No path vs #2 Alabama.
MAAC auto-bid. No path vs Duke.
Elite defense (nation's best). Sampson's most experienced squad.
Rugged SEC defense. Rick Barnes March pedigree.
Mark Pope year 2. Talented but freshman-heavy. R64 upset risk.
Painter's most complete team. Interior dominant, solid FT shooting.
Brownell's best squad. ACC battles make them tournament-ready.
Illinois guards + frontcourt depth. Tournament experienced squad.
Mick Cronin's gritty defense. Big Ten battle-tested.
Few's talented squad, last WCC season. Offense-first, suspect defense.
McCollum's most experienced team. MVC powerhouse. Classic 9-seed trap.
Mountain West contender. Surprising R64 upset ability.
Rodney Terry year 4. SEC newcomer finding its footing. 11-seed value.
Will Wade's system. 12-seed upset threat vs Clemson.
Veteran coach, solid mid-major program.
Sun Belt champion. Troy won title game vs Georgia Southern.
SoCon team. No realistic path vs Tennessee.
OVC auto-bid. No path vs Houston.
Defending champions. Golden's best team. Nation's best AdjEM.
Rick Pitino's St. John's resurgence. Guard-dominated attack.
Texas Tech defense DNA. Slow pace, elite D. Hard to beat.
Willard's most talented Maryland squad. Physical Big Ten squad.
Penny's best recruiting class. Transition-first attack.
SEC-toughened. Dennis Gates builds culture. Dangerous 6-seed.
Bill Self's tournament machine. Down year by KU standards.
Three-peat attempt. Slightly down year. Hurley always adjusts.
SEC newcomer, learning on the fly. Still dangerous in R64.
Calipari year 2 at Arkansas. Recruiting talent arriving. 10-seed value.
MWC sleeper. Medved's system players. Dangerous 11-seed.
WAC champion. Physical team that causes problems for slower 5-seeds.
GCU program rising. Bryce Drew's system. Long shot vs Maryland.
CAA champion. CAA teams always competitive vs 3-seeds.
Summit auto-bid. Respectable but outmatched vs St. John's.
MEAC auto-bid. No realistic path vs Florida.
